transcript:
Agent: Thank you for calling First National Bank, this is Sarah speaking. How can I help you today?

Customer: Hi Sarah, I'm calling about my mortgage application. I submitted it two weeks ago and haven't heard back.

Agent: I'd be happy to help you with that. Can I have your application reference number?

Customer: Yes, it's MRT-2024-0567.

Agent: Thank you. Let me pull that up... I can see your application is currently in the underwriting review stage. The delay is due to a pending property appraisal.

Customer: Oh, I see. When can I expect the appraisal to be completed?

Agent: Typically appraisals take 5-7 business days. Yours was ordered 3 days ago, so you should hear back within the next week. Once the appraisal is complete, the underwriter will finalize the review.

Customer: That's helpful. I also wanted to ask about the interest rate. I was quoted 6.5% but I've seen rates dropping recently.

Agent: Great question. Your rate is locked at 6.5% for 60 days from your application date. However, if rates drop significantly before closing, we do offer a float-down option. I can have our mortgage specialist reach out to discuss your options.

Customer: Yes, please do that. Also, I need to update my employment information. I recently got a promotion.

Agent: Congratulations! I'll note that on your file. You'll need to submit an updated employment verification letter. I'll send you an email with the details on what's needed.

Customer: Perfect. One more thing - what documents do I need for closing?

Agent: For closing, you'll need: government-issued ID, proof of homeowner's insurance, final employment verification, and a cashier's check for closing costs. I'll include a complete checklist in the email.

Customer: Thank you so much, Sarah. You've been very helpful.

Agent: You're welcome! Is there anything else I can help you with today?

Customer: No, that's everything. Thank you.

Agent: Thank you for calling First National Bank. Have a great day!
follow_up_actions: Schedule mortgage specialist callback for rate discussion
